title: GS Group Provides Free AI Tools to SMEs, Bridging AI Gap - 조선일보
content: GS's AI agent AIR and MISO platform, developed with on-site expertise, now used by 130+ SMEs GS Group is actively building an "AI coexistence ecosystem" by providing its self-developed AI platform and agents to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) at no cost. The conglomerate aims to bridge the AI gap between large corporations and smaller businesses by sharing its AI transformation (AX) expertise, honed over years of on-site experience. GS is pursuing this openness based on the strategic judgment that when SMEs adopt AI, a virtuous cycle of accumulating on-site data and advancing technological sophistication will emerge. Beyond merely enhancing its corporate image, the company believes that elevating the overall AI competitiveness of the industrial ecosystem will ultimately benefit GS in the long term. **◇AI Agent Developed by On-Site Employees, Provided Free to 130 SMEs** The safety management AI agent "AIR (Air)," which GS Group is distributing to SMEs free of charge, was conceived and developed by GS Power’s on-site employees. At the "3rd GS Group Hackathon" in 2024, five safety and machinery field practitioners proposed the idea and developed it using GS’s proprietary AX platform "MISO (MISO)" without coding. By simply inputting a task name and brief description, the generative AI automatically generates work processes, potential risk factors, risk levels, and preventive measures. Designed for small workplaces lacking safety management personnel and expertise, the solution enables systematic safety management. GS Power integrated AIR into its internal systems and refined the solution based on on-site data. As a result, the time required for risk assessments—previously conducted manually by cross-referencing the Occupational Safety and Health Act and manuals—was reduced to one-tenth of the original duration. Uniform evaluation quality is now maintained regardless of the assessor’s skill level, allowing more time to be allocated to actual on-site safety inspections rather than repetitive documentation. Over 130 small business sites currently utilize AIR, and GS Group plans to expand its distribution further. A GS Group official stated, "AIR is an AX case that prioritizes the field over technology. We hope SMEs will directly experience the benefits of AI-based safety management and that this will substantially reduce safety gaps in industrial sites." This initiative aligns with Chairman Huh Tae-soo’s emphasis on "AI coexistence." He has consistently stressed, "In the digital era, fostering an ecosystem—not going solo—is essential for business." He has encouraged actively sharing GS’s AI assets to support mid-sized and small companies struggling to secure talent. **◇‘MISO’ Platform with 10,000 Tools, Transferring Know-How to SMEs** The platform "MISO," which gave birth to AIR, is a core asset of GS’s coexistence strategy. MISO allows non-technical employees to implement webpages or business tools simply by conversing with the system. It is designed to securely update company data and freely apply AI models optimized for on-site conditions. Currently, GS employees have developed over 10,000 tools using MISO, with 140 of them integrated into affiliate systems and utilized across production, logistics, and customer data analysis. By sharing MISO’s know-how with SMEs that lack IT infrastructure, GS aims to lower the entry barrier to AI transformation regardless of company size. GS Group’s achievements have also been recognized externally. In January of this year, GS Power was selected as an "Outstanding Case for Spreading Process Safety Management (PSM) Safety Culture" and received the Minister of Employment and Labor Award. The company was highly evaluated not only for enhancing the efficiency and quality of safety management through AI but also for its efforts to expand these solutions to external workplaces. A GS official remarked, "Cases where large corporations open their field-verified AI assets are still rare. GS’s approach could become a new model for AI coexistence between large and small businesses."

title: Hutchinson bets on gravel racing with Touareg Race, Caracal Race GS, and tires up to 50 mm - Brujulabike.com
content: The French company Hutchinson has just launched a deep update of its gravel tire range. It introduces new models, technologies inherited from road cycling, and a clear focus on modern competition. The launch revolves around three tires: Touareg Race, Touareg Race GS, and Caracal Race GS, with a very defined focus on efficiency, control, and adaptation to different racing scenarios. Competitive gravel has evolved into a terrain where data, energy efficiency, and details make real differences, and Hutchinson proposes a solution based on combining tires instead of seeking a single tire for everything. The development of the new Touareg Race starts from a known base but incorporates key elements from the brand's fastest model, the Hutchinson Caracal Race. The goal is to maintain the versatility of the Touareg tread while reducing energy losses and improving pure performance. The use of the Mach Tread 3.0 compound introduces measurable changes in dynamic behavior. According to the provided data, it increases rebound capacity by 25% and tear resistance by 10%, which translates to less energy dissipation and greater durability in aggressive conditions. This is complemented by the SwiftEasy casing, which reduces weight and improves controlled tire deformation, a key factor in maintaining stability without penalizing rolling resistance. The result is a tire that starts at 480 grams and, according to the brand, improves efficiency by 35% compared to the previous Hardskin version. In practical terms, this means a more finely tuned balance between three critical variables in competitive gravel: rolling resistance, structural control of the casing, and grip in fast corners. Hutchinson also proposes a clear usage configuration. The Touareg Race is positioned as a front tire to maximize control and precision, combined with a Hutchinson Caracal Race rear tire focused on speed. According to the brand itself, this combination covers approximately 90% of the usual scenarios in gravel races. This tire is available in 40, 45, and 50 mm, and the last size reflects an increasingly established trend in the segment. The other major novelty is the arrival of GridSkin technology in gravel. It consists of a reinforcement layer in the form of a woven mesh that specifically acts on the tire's sidewalls, a critical area in rocky or aggressive conditions. Hutchinson Touareg Race GS and Hutchinson Caracal Race GS incorporate this solution, which aims to isolate cuts and tears without adding significant penalties in weight or rolling resistance. The approach is clear: to offer a more robust option for races where reliability is as crucial as speed. This type of reinforcement responds to a reality of current gravel, where many events combine fast sections with technical areas that push the integrity of the tire to the limit. Another relevant move is the complete expansion to 50 mm sections across the entire range. This is not limited to the Touareg Race but also includes the Caracal Race, which will be available in this width for the first time. This decision is directly connected to the evolution of materials in gravel. Wider rims and lower pressures are pushing towards tires with greater volume, which improve comfort, traction, and efficiency on uneven terrain. The entire range maintains tubeless ready construction and compatibility with modern wider internal rims, a standard already established in competition. With this launch, Hutchinson not only expands its catalog but also redefines its approach to competitive gravel, betting on a modular system based on tire combinations and adaptation to the terrain. For the brand, the key is not in a single universal model but in optimizing each setup according to the actual racing conditions. Subscribe to our newsletter and receive all our news. Coinbase pays us for certain activity generated through this link. Prices displayed are informational. Goldman Sachs (GS) is one of the stocks most watched by Zacks.com visitors lately. So, it might be a good idea to review some of the factors that might affect the near-term performance of the stock. Shares of this investment bank have returned +9.5% over the past month versus the Zacks S&P 500 composite's +12.2% change. The Zacks Financial - Investment Bank industry, to which Goldman belongs, has gained 12% over this period. Now the key question is: Where could the stock be headed in the near term? While media releases or rumors about a substantial change in a company's business prospects usually make its stock 'trending' and lead to an immediate price change, there are always some fundamental facts that eventually dominate the buy-and-hold decision-making. Here at Zacks, we prioritize appraising the change in the projection of a company's future earnings over anything else. That's because we believe the present value of its future stream of earnings is what determines the fair value for its stock. We essentially look at how sell-side analysts covering the stock are revising their earnings estimates to reflect the impact of the latest business trends. And if earnings estimates go up for a company, the fair value for its stock goes up. A higher fair value than the current market price drives investors' interest in buying the stock, leading to its price moving higher. This is why empirical research shows a strong correlation between trends in earnings estimate revisions and near-term stock price movements. Goldman is expected to post earnings of $13.71 per share for the current quarter, representing a year-over-year change of +25.7%. Over the last 30 days, the Zacks Consensus Estimate has changed -2.5%. For the current fiscal year, the consensus earnings estimate of $59.53 points to a change of +16% from the prior year. Over the last 30 days, this estimate has changed +3.5%. For the next fiscal year, the consensus earnings estimate of $65.79 indicates a change of +10.5% from what Goldman is expected to report a year ago. title: WJT Returns to Dushanbe with Unmissable GS Action - IJF
content: Nestled in Central Asia, Tajikistan is a country of dramatic mountains, rich history and a deep-rooted sporting culture. Judo holds a special place in society, with growing popularity and strong institutional support. In recent years, the nation has made significant strides on the international stage, highlighted by its two Olympic judo medals at the Paris 2024 Olympic Games. In the French capital, Tajik athletes secured two bronze medals in a landmark moment for the nation. The country had already won an Olympic bronze medal in 2008 at the Beijing Games. Those achievements have accelerated investment in infrastructure and athlete development, transforming the country into a rising force in global judo. At the heart of this movement lies Dushanbe, a vibrant and rapidly developing capital. Known for its wide boulevards and welcoming atmosphere, Dushanbe has become a recognised hub for international judo events. The Kasri Tennis arena, which has hosted competitions for several years, will once again provide the stage for world-class action. With only two events remaining before the start of Olympic qualification, in Mongolia next month, the Dushanbe Grand Slam promises to deliver high stakes and high intensity. For athletes, every contest matters. For fans, it is an opportunity to witness some of the finest judoka in the world competing with full commitment. And if there is one thing that defines Dushanbe, it is its atmosphere. The Tajik crowd is among the most passionate and energetic on the circuit. Long before the first contest begins, spectators gather in and around the arena, creating a unique buzz that builds throughout the day. Once the action begins, the energy is constant, every ippon celebrated, every effort recognised. For the 2026 edition, nearly 250 athletes from 34 nations are expected to compete. The diversity of participants ensures a rich and unpredictable competition, where emerging talent meets established stars. If you are looking for spectacular judo, you will find it. If you are looking for ippon, you will see it, and if you are looking for an unforgettable atmosphere, Dushanbe will deliver. All the ingredients are in place for a memorable event. title: Assessing Goldman Sachs (GS) Valuation After Strong 1 Year Shareholder Returns - simplywall.st
content:
Goldman Sachs Group (GS) has been drawing attention after recent share price moves, with the stock last closing at US$926.55 and showing mixed returns over the past month and past 3 months. Despite a 1.2% single day share price decline, the recent 15.4% 1 month share price return and strong 1 year total shareholder return of 72.29% suggest momentum has generally been building over time. If Goldman Sachs has you thinking about financials more broadly, it can be useful to see which companies owners are still closely involved in running. Now is a good time to broaden your search and check out the 18 top founder-led companies With GS trading near its US$935 analyst price target and carrying a value score of 3, the key question is whether that 72.29% 1 year return still leaves upside on the table or if the market is already pricing in future growth. Goldman Sachs Group's most followed narrative pegs fair value at about $934 per share, slightly above the last close at $926.55, which implies only a modest valuation gap and focuses attention on the earnings engine behind that number. Record growth and momentum in Asset & Wealth Management, including strong fee-based net inflows for 30 consecutive quarters and rising demand for alternative assets from high-net-worth and institutional clients, are shifting the revenue mix toward less volatile, high-margin streams, which supports higher and more durable net margins. Want to see what kind of revenue glide path and margin profile can support that fair value with only a small discount to today’s price? The narrative leans heavily on steady fee income, incremental margin expansion and a tighter share count to justify its earnings path and the P/E needed to get there, but keeps one key assumption in the background for readers willing to go deeper. Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts. However, you also need to weigh ongoing regulatory uncertainty and intense talent competition, either of which could pressure margins and challenge the current fair value story. Find out about the key risks to this Goldman Sachs Group narrative. The narrative and analyst targets suggest Goldman Sachs is roughly fairly priced around $934, but the current P/E of 16.6x tells a slightly different story. It sits well below the US Capital Markets industry on 42.1x, the peer average of 26.8x, and even the 17.8x fair ratio estimate. That gap hints the market is either giving you a margin of safety or pricing in risks that the consensus narrative is underplaying. Which side of that trade do you think is closer to reality?
